channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: URGENT! Large hole in concrete manhole cover in pavement, 3rd July
item.description:
URGENT! Concrete manhole cover outside nos. 19a/19b Robertson Street, SW8 3TX has a LARGE HOLE which is large enough for an adult foot to go through, easy for a child's foot. URGENT ATTENTION NEEDED. The pavement is not well lit at night so even more DANGEROUS then.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Robertson Street, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Signage and Road Markings on roundabout, 3rd July
item.description:
Heading south down A10 to M25 yesterday. Not familiar with A10. Sign for M25 north of roundabout with A121 / B128. However there is NOTHING to help you get in the correct lane on the roundabout. No roadmarkings for M25, No roadsigns helping you get off. Result: me doing laps of the roundabout trying to get orientated, getting hooted at by other traffic wondering what this idiot is doing! Something should be done. Thank you for reading!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Waltham Cross, Broxbourne
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mattress and black bags with rubbish, 3rd July
item.description:
Again, last 10 days, 3x dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bath Passage, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Communual area over grown, 3rd July
item.description:
Not had grass cut for 2 months now
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westhorne Avenue, Eltham

item.title: Sanato Remedio food waste 3rd July, 3rd July
item.description:
Food waste from Santo Remedio strewn across Shand street again this morning Proper waste containment is needed for this restaurant

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 136-148, Tooley Street, The Borough, Greater London

item.title: Street sign unreadable, 3rd July
item.description:
This was reported in Feb 2026 ref 88811189 - advised could take a couple of months due to bulk orders 5 months later still not replaced - kindly advise what the delay is or reason for not being rectified
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dorcis Avenue, West Heath, Greater London

item.description:
Furniture left on bridge

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Halstow Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden of the home next door to 58 Camden st (the green door corner shop), 3rd July
item.description:
I have already written directly to the neighbour to raise this and request treatment. However, I am a leaseholder in my building, where the ground floor flat is owned by Camden Council, and I understand the neighbouring property may also fall under the Council’s ownership or management. Given this, I wanted to ask whether the Council can assist, as any spread of the knotweed will directly affect a property Camden effectively owns. Japanese knotweed is taken seriously under the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981, as it is an offence to allow it to spread into the wild, and its roots can cause significant damage to walls and foundations if left untreated. Could you please confirm the ownership status of the neighbouring property, and let me know what support or action the Council can offer given the direct impact on Camden’s own property interests? Many thanks, Benjamin Lennox 07990794541

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Camden Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: New tree pushed to an angle, 3rd July
item.description:
I've had a look at the little tree, and it's still alive, but the stake and wire mesh have been knocked out of the ground and I can't get them back into position. It needs your help, now! It's on the corner of Hamond Square and Hoxton Street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hemsworth Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip in Chequers Way, 3rd July
item.description:
These items have been dumped in Chequers Way near the junction with Pasteur Gardens again!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pasteur Gardens, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can parked on entire pavement and double yellow lines, 3rd July
item.description:
Large builder’s van repeatedly parked on double yellows and pavement at top of Grovelands close, where it meets Camberwell Grove. This is forcing pedestrians to dangerously walk in road at junction, risking being hit by vehicles turning in. Means people wheeling prams or using wheelchairs cannot pass. Also danger to pedestrians crossing top of road on Camberwell grove. Vehicle should be parked in building’s large driveway.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-8, Grovelands Close, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley dumped, 3rd July
item.description:
Trolley has been dumped over a week (anti social behaviour) & could potentially cause an accident or damage to vehicles parked or driving by.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pengarth Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collection of poo seaton road, 3rd July
item.description:
Multiple bags of poo near 1 seaton road Poo next to yellow salt box. This is constant.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seaton Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip Hazard from Cracked Pavement Slabs, 3rd July
item.description:
In the photo, a number of slabs (the worst are marked with the red crosses) are raised and/or cracked. Some have voids underneath. These slabs represent a trip hazard to pedestrians. Also, in periods of rainfall when the voids fill up, walking over the slabs often results in spurts of muddy water hitting pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 59, Dulverton Road, New Eltham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Fly tipping in this location

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kimbolton Close, Hither Green, Greater London

item.description:
